name: Beehiiv API
what_it_does: Newsletter platform API for managing Beehiiv publications programmatically. Supports subscriber CRUD operations, post creation and management, subscription tier management, automation triggers, referral program access, and publication analytics. Designed for newsletter operators and growth hackers building on top of Beehiiv's creator infrastructure.
use_cases: Sync subscribers between Beehiiv and a CRM or data warehouse via bulk import/export
Automatically create and publish newsletter posts from a content pipeline or AI agent
Trigger subscriber welcome sequences or segment-based automations programmatically
Retrieve subscriber engagement data (opens, clicks) for churn prediction models
Manage multiple Beehiiv publications from a single integration
not_for: Transactional email delivery (Beehiiv is for newsletters, not triggered transactional messages)
Building a fully custom email sending infrastructure (Beehiiv is a managed platform)
Accessing subscribers of a publication you don't own or have API access to
best_when: You're an operator of a Beehiiv newsletter and want to automate subscriber management, content publishing, or integrate Beehiiv data into external analytics or CRM systems.
avoid_when: You need transactional email, advanced segmentation with arbitrary custom logic, or a platform that allows full white-labeling of the sending infrastructure.

auth.notes: Bearer token in Authorization header. API key is scoped to a specific Beehiiv account and all publications within it. No per-publication key granularity. Keys created from the Beehiiv dashboard under API settings.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access requires a paid Beehiiv plan. API is not available on the free Launch plan.
pricing.paid_tiers: Grow ($42/mo): API access included. Scale ($84/mo): higher limits + advanced features. Enterprise: custom.
pricing.notes: API availability is gated behind paid plans. Rate limits scale with plan tier. For programmatic publishing at volume, Scale plan is recommended.

performance.rate_limits: 100 requests per 60 seconds per API key. Bulk subscriber import has separate limits (up to 1,000 subscribers per request).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors return standard HTTP codes with JSON detail. Validation errors include field names. Rate limit responses include Retry-After header. Some edge case errors lack sufficient context.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Subscriber creation is idempotent by email address — re-submitting an existing subscriber email updates rather than duplicates. Post creation is not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: API key has no publication-level scoping — a single compromised key exposes all publications on the account
Post publishing is a two-step process (create draft, then publish) — agents that only call create will leave posts as drafts
Subscriber import via API does not trigger welcome automations by default; requires explicit automation_trigger parameter
Webhooks for subscriber events have no signature verification in older integrations — validate source IP if security is critical
Analytics data (opens, clicks) is only available for posts sent on Scale plan or higher; agents relying on engagement data will get empty responses on Grow plan

security_score_components.security_notes: Single API key with no scope granularity is a risk for multi-publication operators. GDPR compliance is platform-level but agents managing subscriber PII must implement their own data minimization policies.
